raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting a complaint regarding how American Express handled a dispute involving a {$7500.00} charge for a coaching program that was materially misrepresented and ultimately not delivered. 

I have been an American Express cardmember for 36 years and have rarely needed to file disputes. This is not a routine complaint, but a significant issue involving misrepresentation and loss of access to services. 

I purchased a coaching program from a company called XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Prior to purchasing, I clearly explained that I have 17 years of experience in XXXX XXXX and confirmed with multiple representatives that the program was appropriate for an XXXX XXXX I was assured that it was. 

After gaining access, it became clear that the program was basic and designed for beginners. I raised this repeatedly with my assigned coach, who instructed me to skip from module to module in an attempt to find content that applied to my level. I was directed through multiple modules ( up to module XXXX ), but the material remained introductory and not as represented. 

The live coaching calls further confirmed this, as they were geared toward beginners with little to no experience. 

I later spoke with the founder, who acknowledged that the program was not a fit for me and attempted to move me into a completely different program that I did not purchase. 

After I raised concerns and disputed the charge, the company removed my access to all components of the program, including coaching, XXXX  channels, and XXXX groups. At that point, I no longer had access to any services, despite having paid {$7500.00}. 

I submitted extensive documentation to American Express, including : * Evidence of misrepresentation * Documentation showing the level of the program did not match what was sold * Proof that I was removed from access after disputing * A detailed explanation of my experience Despite this, American Express denied my dispute, relying solely on the merchants contract and not addressing the underlying issue of misrepresentation and non-delivery of services. 

This is not a matter of dissatisfaction. This is a case where : * The service was misrepresented at the time of sale * The service delivered did not match what was promised * Access to the service was revoked after the dispute American Express failed to properly investigate these facts and did not provide the level of consumer protection I expected after 36 years as a cardmember. 

I am requesting that this dispute be reviewed again based on the full facts and that appropriate action be taken.
